hotspots.label: Narayanganj Textile-Tannery Discharge
hotspots.description: Narayanganj hosts Bangladesh's densest textile and former tannery cluster. Hundreds of dyeing/washing units discharge directly into the Shitalakshya. Water turns visibly colored (red, blue, black) depending on dyeing schedules. DoE has documented dissolved oxygen below 1 mg/L in dry season.

hotspots.label: Gazipur-Tongi Industrial Effluent
hotspots.description: Gazipur district hosts 1,000+ garment and textile factories. Industrial wastewater from Tongi, Konabari, and Sreepur industrial estates flows into the Turag and its tributaries. One of South Asia's densest manufacturing clusters. Spectral signatures show chronic pollution year-round.

hotspots.label: Chittagong Karnaphuli Industrial Zone
hotspots.description: The Karnaphuli River through Chittagong receives effluent from ship-breaking yards (Sitakunda), petrochemical facilities, steel re-rolling mills, and Chittagong EPZ. Heavy metals (Cr, Pb, Cd) documented in sediments. Oil sheen visible on satellite imagery near port areas.

hotspots.label: Khulna-Mongla Industrial & Shrimp Runoff
hotspots.description: Khulna's Rupsha and Bhairab rivers receive effluent from jute mills, seafood processing plants, Khulna Shipyard, and extensive shrimp farm drainage. Nutrient overloading from shrimp aquaculture causes seasonal algal blooms. Proximity to the Sundarbans makes this an ecologically critical zone.

hotspots.label: Narsingdi Handloom Dyeing Discharge
hotspots.description: Narsingdi is one of Bangladesh's oldest handloom and powerloom textile centers. Hundreds of informal dyeing and washing units operate along tributaries flowing into the Meghna system. Most units lack any effluent treatment. The Arialkha river and local khals carry concentrated dye waste year-round.

hotspots.label: Savar-Hemayetpur Tannery Relocation Zone
hotspots.description: Since 2017, ~150 Hazaribagh tanneries were relocated to Savar Leather Industrial City. However, the Central Effluent Treatment Plant (CETP) remains non-functional. Tanneries discharge untreated chromium-laden effluent into the Dhaleshwari River tributary. DoE monitoring confirms Cr6+ levels far exceeding limits.

data_source: Generated from real OSM factory clusters + spectral index estimation
